name: Jimmy McPartland
abstract: James Dugald "Jimmy" McPartland (March 15, 1907 – March 13, 1991) was an American cornetist. He worked with Eddie Condon, Art Hodes, Gene Krupa, Benny Goodman, Jack Teagarden, and Tommy Dorsey, often leading his own bands.

about_quotes.speaker: Keith Ingham
about_quotes.evidence: Marian McPartland was probably the first, because she married the trumpet player Jimmy McPartland, who was a GI I guess.
about_quotes.doc_title: Keith Ingham interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Teagarden
about_quotes.evidence: And then I went with, uh, Benny Pollock. Which at that time had a wonderful band for a big band... There was a little kid with us, uh, the name of Benny Goodman. In those days he was about 18 years old. Jimmy McPartland and, uh, Bud Freeman.

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Bauduc and Nappy Lamare
about_quotes.evidence: Benny Pollock had the most wonderful band I ever heard in my life. That was including Benny Goodman in the band. They had Jack Teague on and Jimmy McPartland and Big Braider's on piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Bauduc and Nappy Lamare Interview, 1959-09-09

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: The line-up was Jimmy McPartland, Bud Freeman, on tenor, and on piano we had -- who was playing drums -- George Wettling was playing drums, Dick Cary and we had Milt Hinton.

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: I put the band together. ... I had Jimmy McPartland playing the trumpet for the Sal Mineo before he got the magic horn
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Gordon
about_quotes.evidence: I used to play with ... Jimmy McPartland. That was just little stints, eight weeks here, maybe sixteen weeks there
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Gordon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 12, 1997
